Document:
# Core Rule 8 - Medium Roleplay (MRP)
Station 7 is a Medium Roleplay (MRP) server. This means you are expected to play a character — not just a player controlling a body.
## What MRP Means at Station7
You should have a character with a role, a purpose, and a reason to be on the station. Your character knows what their job is and operates within the bounds of that job and their experience. A chef knows how to cook. They do not know how to breach the Captain's quarters or perform surgery. Play what your character would reasonably know and do.
You do not need to be serious all the time. Humorous and lighthearted characters are welcome, as long as they still make sense within the context of a functioning space station. A clown being a clown is fine. A chef suddenly deciding to demolish the bridge for no reason is not.
## What This Looks Like In Practice
- Play your role. A chef cooks. An engineer fixes things. You can interact with the rest of the station, but your job is your anchor. - Your character knows what they know. A janitor wasn't trained in surgery. A botanist doesn't inherently know how to hack an airlock. - React to the round as your character would, not as a player who has seen it all before. - Antagonists have more freedom, but should still have some reason behind their actions.
## What MRP Is Not
MRP is not HRP (High Roleplay). You are not required to write paragraphs of emotes or maintain a deep backstory. You just need to be a person on a space station doing your job, reacting to the world around you, and not breaking immersion for others without cause.
